title: ACME-sorbitol (ACMEsorb) Fixation and Mechanical Dissociation of Whole Soft-Bodied Cnidarians
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4tahm","text":"ACME (Acetic-Methanol) maceration is a tissue dissociation and fixation method described by Jordi Solana’s group that preserves both cell morphology and RNA integrity, and is compatible with single-cell transcriptomics (scRNA-seq) (García-Castro et al., 2021). ACME has been tested on various organisms, including freshwater planarians (Dugesia japonica), annelids (Pristina leidyi), snails (Limnaea stagnalis), vertebrate embryos (Mus musculus and Danio rerio), terrestrial arthropods (Parasteatoda tepidariorum embryos and Drosophila melanogaster larvae), and the starlet sea anemone (Nematostella vectensis), which inhabits brackish waters (García-Castro et al., 2021).","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33},{"style":"italic","offset":337,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":366,"length":15},{"style":"italic","offset":392,"length":17},{"style":"italic","offset":432,"length":12},{"style":"italic","offset":449,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":487,"length":25},{"style":"italic","offset":525,"length":23},{"style":"italic","offset":587,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hujf","text":"However, for marine organisms, this protocol proved suboptimal in our hands, as we observed cells bursting immediately upon contact with the fixative solution—presumably due to the hypo-osmolarity of the fixative relative to seawater. To compensate for the osmolarity and preserve cell integrity during fixation, we replaced the water (or 1× PBS) fraction in the original ACME formulation with a 0.8 M sorbitol solution (Najle et al., 2023).","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2etgv","text":"This modified version, which we named ACMEsorb, was successfully applied for single-cell atlasing of various marine species. ACMEsorb fixation of whole organisms, followed by cell dissociation, is the preferred method, as it minimises cellular stress and reduces viability biases observed during the dissociation of fresh samples.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":38,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a92tt","text":"Dissociation post-fixation can be performed either mechanically, as described here, or enzymatically (a protocol using cold-protease dissociation after ACMEsorb fixation is provided separately). In this protocol, we use the gentleMACSTM Octo Dissociator with a custom program (see below) to fix and dissociate whole specimens of the starlet sea anemone N. vectensis. This protocol has also been successfully applied to three other sea anemone species (Exaiptasia diaphana, Diadumene lineata, and Metridium senile), with similar results.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":234,"length":2},{"style":"italic","offset":353,"length":12},{"style":"italic","offset":452,"length":19},{"style":"italic","offset":473,"length":17},{"style":"italic","offset":496,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"nf9qu","text":"Use ultrapure reagents (free of nucleases) and keep your pipettes and workbench clean and free of RNases (clean surfaces with RNase Zap or similar) throughout the whole experiment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"43a10","text":"Use nuclease-free water (for example, Sigma-Aldrich #W4502-1L) for all the solutions and buffers. During sample prep, keep all the reagents and samples on ice unless otherwise indicated in the protocols. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"897em","text":"ACMEsorb-fixed cells are very resistant to mechanical stress. This is the reason for the high centrifugation speed (2500 x g) used after fixation, which is intended to maximize cell recovery. However, this speed can be optimized (reduced) to avoid cell clumping, provided this is observed after thoroughly resuspending the pellet.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9h2jg","text":"Prepare an appropriate volume of ACMEsorb solution and Resuspension Buffer 1 (RB1) based on the number of samples to be processed. Refer to the 'Materials' section for the composition of each solution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":33,"length":17},{"style":"bold","offset":54,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3406l","text":"Volumes of fixative solution and buffers are orientative and should be adjusted according to the amount of material. As a reference, the volumes specified here were used for 3 N. vectensis specimens of ~1cm each.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":176,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Avoid vortexing to preserve enzyme activity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a895u","text":"Prepare fresh or keep   until use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":22,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"3plo0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"321dt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"","unit":32}},"1":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"-20","unit":10}},"2":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"","unit":32}},"3":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"","unit":165}}}}
steps: Transfer animal(s) to a small Petri dish or 6-well plate, in a minimum volume of artificial seawater (ASW) at the corresponding salinity depending on the species. Here, we use 14 ppm (1.4 g/L) Red Sea Salts for N. vectensis .
Quickly rinse the animals twice with 2 mL Nv-Calcium and Magnesium-free seawater (Nv-CMFSW).
Remove as much Nv-CMFSW as possible, and add 3 mL of freshly made ACMEsorb solution of the following composition: 1950 μl 1.2M sorbitol 300 μl glycerol 300 μl glacial acetic acid 450 μl methanol
Immediately chop the animals into 3-4 mm pieces using a scalpel or a razor blade.
Using a wide bore tip or a plastic Pasteur pipette, transfer the tissue pìeces in ACMEsorb to a gentleMACS C tube.

After GentleMACS program is finished, evaluate the dissociation efficiency. If big chunks or undissociated tissue are still visible by the end of the procedure, try to aid dissociation by pipetting with a P1000 set at 300 μl.
Once fixation/dissociation is completed, transfer the sample to a 5 mL protein LoBind tube using a P1000 and filtering through a 70 μm strainer.
Remove ¾ ( 2 mL ) supernatant and add an equal volume of Resuspension Buffer 1 (RB1).
Gently resuspend the cell pellet by pipetting up and down 5 times with a P1000 set at 300 μl.
Carefully remove as much supernatant as possible and thoroughly resuspend the pellet with 900 µL RB1.
Add 100 µL DMSO, invert the tube three times to mix and freeze at -80 °C using a Nalgene® Mr Frosty freezing container following the recommendations of the manufacturer.

Place the tube in the gentleMACS dissociator, and run the following pre-set program: Step Speed (rpm) Time (hh:mm:ss) 1 Initial incubation (fixation) 40 00:20:00 2 Dissociation & fixation (repeat 2x) 40 00:10:00 3 300 00:00:10 4 -800 00:00:05 5 600 00:00:10 6 -600 00:00:05 7 120 00:00:15 GentleMACS program 'BCA001' The full fixation/dissociation program lasts for 42.5 min.
Collet the cells by 2500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes, in a swing-bucket rotor.
Collet the cells by 2500 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes, in a swing-bucket rotor.
creator: Sebastián R. Najle
